context_pointers.vague_reference: ensure the output order matches the internal sorting convention used in the package_selector module
context_pointers.actual_meaning: The packages must be sorted lexicographically by their full package identifier string.

context_pointers.vague_reference: apply the boolean flag for development versions using the same logic as seen in the version_utils aggregator
context_pointers.actual_meaning: The dev field must be true if the package version contains a prefix like 'dev-', 'edge-', or matches a specific dev-pattern regex defined in version_utils.

oracle_data.fallback_response: I don't have more detail on that — check the codebase or the existing test cases for the exact behavior.

feature1_package_selection.json.description: Select packages from configured repositories using requested package names, dependency traversal, stability, type, and blacklist options.

cases.expected_output:
selected_count=6
package=vendor/project-alpha-1.2.3.0
package=vendor/project-alpha-dev-main
package=vendor/project-beta-1.2.3.0
package=vendor/project-beta-dev-main
package=vendor/project-gamma-3.4.5.0
package=vendor/project-gamma-4.5.6.0

feature2_download_source_cleaning.json.description: Remove package download sources whose hosts match configured stripping rules while preserving sources that remain allowed.

cases.expected_output:
remaining_count=4
package=beta dist=http://192.168.0.2/output/dist/beta.zip source=null
package=gamma dist=http://192.168.0.1/output/dist/gamma.zip source=http://192.168.1.1/gamma.git
package=delta dist=http://example.org/output/dist/delta.zip source=http://source.example.org/delta.git
package=epsilon dist=http://[abcd::]/output/dist/epsilon.zip source=null

remaining_count=5
package=alpha dist=http://127.0.0.1/output/dist/alpha.zip source=null
package=beta dist=null source=http://localhost/beta.git
package=gamma dist=null source=http://192.168.1.1/gamma.git
package=delta dist=http://example.org/output/dist/delta.zip source=http://source.example.org/delta.git
package=epsilon dist=http://[abcd::]/output/dist/epsilon.zip source=http://[::1]/epsilon.git

feature3_metadata_generation.json.description: Generate repository metadata files with include files, provider entries, notification endpoints, homepage-relative URLs, and compact version metadata.

feature4_existing_metadata_loading.json.description: Load packages from an existing generated metadata directory and omit packages that are scheduled to be regenerated.

cases.expected_output:
loaded_count=2
package=vendor/name version=1.0 dev=false
package=vendor/name version=dev-master dev=true

feature5_catalog_rendering.json.description: Render a browseable package catalog page containing title, package links, dependency links, and abandoned package notices.

cases.expected_output:
title=dummy root package
package_anchor=present
dependency_anchor=absent
abandoned_notice=absent
abandoned_detail=none

title=dummy root package
package_anchor=present
dependency_anchor=absent
abandoned_notice=present
abandoned_detail=replacement:othervendor/othername

feature6_archive_policy.json.description: Resolve archive storage paths and decide whether a package should be skipped during archive creation.

_constraints.output_rule: Output must start with newline-delimited 'selected_count=N' followed by 'package=<name-version>'. The packages must be sorted by package identifier.

_constraints.input_condition: selection.required_packages contains dependency versions or simple version constraints
_constraints.output_rule: Internal logic must apply dependency resolution and dependency traversal based on 'required_packages' constraints.
When mode is 'all', select all packages matching the filter criteria regardless of individual dependency requirements.
Filter selected packages based on the specified minimum stability level.
Output must start with 'remaining_count=N', then 'package=<name> dist=<url-or-null> source=<url-or-null>' for each remaining package in input order.

_constraints.input_condition: package has no remaining valid download sources after filtering
_constraints.output_rule: The package must be excluded from the output entirely if both dist and source URLs are stripped.

_constraints.input_condition: config.strip-hosts contains patterns like '/local' or IPv4/IPv6 network ranges
_constraints.output_rule: Any URL matching the strip-hosts patterns (local path, local host, private CIDR, IPv6) must be removed.
Output lines must strictly follow: 'metadata_url=...', 'available=...', 'include_file=...', 'include_packages=...', 'p2_versions=...' based on config and packages.
Output must include 'providers_url=...', 'providers=...', and the hash in 'include_file'.
All UTCs must be rooted at the provided homepage path (e.g., metadata_url starts with /sub-dir/).
Command must return 'loaded_count=0' with no package details.

_constraints.input_condition: metadata_state == 'complete' AND package not in package_filter
_constraints.output_rule: Package must be loaded and emitted with 'package=<name> version=<version> dev=<true|false>'.
Output must include title, package_anchor, dependency_anchor, abandoned_notice, and abandoned_detail fields.
abandoned_notice must be 'present', and abandoned_detail must be 'replacement:<name>' or 'no_replacement' if no replacement provided.

_constraints.input_condition: query == 'package' AND package.version is a dev version AND skip-dev is enabled
package provides a virtual package name that is blacklisted

hide.replacement: a configurable list of network patterns to strip
a specific replacement string format or default fallback
